{"action":"opened","number":25568,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/pulls/25568","id":1483937041,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAAnGFs5YcxUR","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/25568","diff_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/25568.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/25568.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/25568","number":25568,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add a property for the stiffness matrix for LagrangesMethod","user":{"login":"shuohaopolyu","id":87934285,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3OTM0Mjg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/87934285?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu","html_url":"https://github.com/shuohaopolyu","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/shuohaopolyu/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* Added a property in LagrangesMethod class for the stiffness matrix computation of dynamical systems
  * The Lagrange method is commonly used for the derivation of governing equations of dynamical systems, while in the lagrange.py we could not find a method to compute the stiffness of the system, which is very important in many analyses. I added a property in the class termed stiffness_matrix, and with this, we can directly generate the system's stiffness.
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

If you want the correct stiffness matrix you should use the linearization tools in mechanics so that the equilbrium point is properly accounted for.
> 
> Furthermore, you haven't provided a use case, examples, or unit tests to show the need for exposing this quantity. Having access to the Jacobian of the equations of motion with respect to the coordinates may have utility, but more info is needed.

Thanks for your comments, Mr. Moore, I will upload some use cases on this in the coming days.

I'm a modal analysis guy working on structural health monitoring, so when I get in touch with the SymPy Lagrangian method, the first thing I would like to do is use it to derive the mass and stiffness of the systems. 

BTW, thank you again for your kind and quick reminder, this is actually the first time I try to contribute to a Python package.  I will try the linearization tools you have mentioned.
